door1.primary_user: Invited friend, on phone, tapping a trusted person's link for the first time
top_tasks: Trust it's real (a friend vouched) and feel it's earned
Get in fast — one strong screen, not a forced tour
Understand in one line what Janata is; deeper slides optional/swipeable

door2.primary_user: Cold / not-invited visitor, no account, no friend behind them
top_tasks: Immediately see real events/centers that are relevant — proof there's something for them
Have that relevance shaped by where they are (city / center), without a hard location gate
Secondary: RSVP to a thing without an account, or realize they want a member invite

problems.problem: Door 2 doesn't exist. A cold visitor is email-gated before seeing any value; the only browse affordance is a tiny 'Discover →' link top-right. Inverts the #1 task (see relevant events first).
Door 1 doesn't exist. An invited friend lands in the same generic funnel — the personal vouch ('Anand invited you'), the single biggest trust signal in the growth loop, is lost.
Invite is modeled as a code typed mid-funnel, not as the door. Contradicts the locked 'the link is the door' hard-gate model.
Email-first ordering forces commitment before the user knows if Janata is for them or which path (member vs RSVP) fits — mixed altitude, intent buried.
No account-less RSVP path. The not-invited dead-end; there's no low-friction funnel and no graceful 'you need an invite' wall.
Auto-logged-in / existing member hitting an invite link would be shown signup — hidden state with wrong outcome.
